meeting_context: Public hearing of the Joint Administrative Zoning and Subdivision Committee on October 12, 2022, addressing development approvals at 339 Franklin St., a one-year vesting tentative map extension at 1411–1495 W El Camino Real, and a lot line adjustment at 890 Central Ave. Actions included approvals subject to conditions and appeal windows.

points.text: The consent calendar, including an item at 2134 Old Middlefield Way, was approved without public comment. Stephanie Williams stated the item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days, concluding the consent calendar portion before proceeding to public hearing items.
points.source: Stephanie Williams : And not not seeing anyone wishing to speak on the consent calendar, So I will go ahead and approve the consent calendar and this item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

points.text: Staff summarized that 339 Franklin St. seeks a planned community permit and development review permit to construct a 500 square foot single-family home, a heritage tree removal permit for one tree, and a lot line adjustment on a 0.17-acre site. The project is CEQA-exempt and consistent with the P19 Downtown Precise Plan and General Plan standards.
points.source: Ella Carraccian : So the project includes a request for a planned community permit and development review permit to construct to the 500 square foot single family home... And it is determination that the project is categorically exempt from the sequel review and also a lock line adjustment... The ... project is consistent with the P19 Downtown Precise Plan... and the proposed project is consistent with the General Plan as it allows up to 35 dwelling units per acre.
points.speaker: Ella Carraccian

points.text: Applicant Jenny Gong stated the existing duplex on the property is uninhabitable and not rentable due to lack of utilities, and asserted that constructing a new two-story single-family home would improve the neighborhood and community.
points.source: Jenny Gong : The current duplex structure that's currently there on the property is not only it's inhabitable but it's not rentable given that as a lack of utility... making of a brand new structure, two-story home, it's going to improve the neighborhood as well as the community.
points.speaker: Jenny Gong

points.text: Staff explained the heritage tree straddling the property line has narrow-angle crotches and lean that create substantial breakage risk, and foundation work would cause nearly 50% root loss, increasing instability. Removal is necessary to avoid damage to adjacent homes and to allow construction to proceed safely.
points.source: Ella Carraccian : ...the tree has kind of like 2 narrow angle crutches near the ground level that are at substantial risk for breakage... foundation construction as a result of this project would result in nearly 50% root loss resulting in an increased tree instability which is kind of like dangerous for both neighbours.
points.speaker: Ella Carraccian

points.text: A condition of approval requires the applicant to remove or modify existing balconies so they do not exceed 150 square feet before submitting to the Building Department, should the project be approved.
points.source: Ella Carraccian : ...one of the conditions... that the applicant is subject to before submission to building department... is to remove or kind of like modify the existing balconies and to not exceed 150 square feet under project site proposal.
points.speaker: Ella Carraccian

points.text: Project complies with P19 Downtown Precise Plan development standards including building height, FAR, setbacks and open space requirements.
points.source: Ella Carraccian : And the project also complies with development and standards, including building hide, FAR setbacks and open space of the downtown.
points.speaker: Ella Carraccian

topics.topic: W El Camino Real extension request

points.text: Staff reported the applicant requested a one-year extension of a previously approved vesting tentative map to create 53 condominium lots and one common lot on a 1.25-acre site at 1411–1495 W El Camino Real (P38 Precise Plan). The proposal is consistent with applicable plans, the Subdivision Map Act, and is CEQA-exempt under Section 15332.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill : The applicant is requesting A1 year extension of a previously approved vesting tentative map to create 53 condominium lots and one condom common lot on an R or on a 1.25 acre project... Staff has also determined that the project is categorically exempt from Sequa pursuant to Section 15332 infill developments of the SEQUEL guidelines.
points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill

points.text: The developer's representative stated the one-year extension is requested to complete construction documents and obtain permits, and indicated willingness to address any questions from the committee.
points.source: Steve Saray : ...we're just requesting for a one year extension and so we can get our construction documents and permits in place.
points.speaker: Steve Saray

points.text: Project found consistent with El Camino Real Precise Plan and categorically exempt from CEQA under Section 15332 infill development guidelines.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill : Site staff has found that the proposed vesting tentative map extension is consistent with the El Camino real precise plan and mixed-use corridor general planned land use designation as well as the subdivision map.
points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill

points.text: Stephanie Williams stated the El Camino Real extension item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days after the committee's action.
points.source: Stephanie Williams : This item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

points.text: Staff presented a lot line adjustment at 890 Central Ave. (northeast corner of Sterling Rd. and Central Ave.) in the R1 district to relocate a lot line between two existing lots on a 0.44-acre site. The proposal meets R1 and low-density land use standards and is CEQA-exempt under Section 15305.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill : ...the applicant is requesting a lot line adjustment to relocate a lot line between two existing lots on a .44 acre project... Staff has also determined that the project is categorically exempt from Sequa pursuant to Section 15305 minor alterations in land use limitations of the Sequa guidelines.
points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill

points.text: The public hearing concluded after actions on 339 Franklin St., 1411–1495 W El Camino Real, and 890 Central Ave., and the meeting adjourned at 4:15 PM following completion of the agenda.
points.source: Stephanie Williams : So we will adjourn this meeting at 4:15 PM.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

points.text: All approved items are appealable to City Council within 10 calendar days of committee decision.
points.source: Stephanie Williams : This entire project is available to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

decisions.text: Subdivision Committee approved the lot line adjustment to combine two lots into one 0.17-acre lot at 339 Franklin St., subject to conditions (unanimous).
decisions.source: Ed Arango : ...motion to approve the lot line adjustment to remove an existing lot line to combine two lots into one lot on .17 acre project site at 339 Franklin St.

decisions.text: Subdivision Committee approved a one-year extension of the vesting tentative map at 1411–1495 W El Camino Real (unanimous).
decisions.source: Stephanie Williams : Aye, that's a unit. Unanimous approval from the subdivision committee.

decisions.text: Subdivision Committee approved the lot line adjustment at 890 Central Ave., subject to conditions and conformance (unanimous).
decisions.source: Ed Arango : I'd like to make a motion to approve the lot line adjustment at 890 Central, subject to the conditions of approval and conformance with the General Plan and Zoning Ordinance.

decisions.text: Stephanie Williams approved the consent calendar item at 2134 Old Middlefield Way; appealable within 10 calendar days.
decisions.source: Stephanie Williams : I will go ahead and approve the consent calendar and this item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.

decisions.text: Stephanie Williams approved the planned community permit, development review permit and heritage tree removal for 339 Franklin Street subject to draft findings and conditions of approval.
decisions.source: Stephanie Williams : And we'll be approving the planned community permit, development review permit and heritage tree removal subject to those draft findings and conditions of approval.
